reason: Both answers reach the same correct verdict and cover the same core points: ES tax (projections, replay, versioning), team-size mismatch, using an append-only ledger for auditability, and considering an existing billing provider before building. Coverage is essentially equivalent. B adds one genuinely sharper, billing-specific insight A lacks - whether to re-rate historical invoices when pricing logic changes, a real gotcha with ES - plus a concrete detail about idempotent ingestion for usage events. A's unique additions (double-entry ledger phrasing, Stripe anecdote) are reasonable but less decision-relevant, and A's heavy texting shorthand reduces clarity. B is marginally more useful and better written.
